```json
{
  "source_gene_1": "Autoregressive code language model evaluation treats source code as a token sequence, trains large next-token predictors on code, and systematically probes them across scales, prompts, and task formats using both open-ended generation and constrained prediction benchmarks.",
  "source_gene_2": "AlphaCode uses a large autoregressive code model to massively sample candidate programs per task, then filters them through compilation and execution checks, clusters them by behavioral similarity, and ranks a small diverse subset likely to generalize.",
  "core_changed_idea": "Instead of systematically probing autoregressive code predictors, AlphaCode shifts from benchmark probing to massive program sampling, filtering, clustering, and diverse ranking for likely generalizable solutions.",
  "dynamics_label": "Speciation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Understanding collaborative software development behavior across many projects.",
  "Mechanism": "Large-scale empirical mining of software repository traces, shifted from pull request acceptance/integration modeling to a before/after design around continuous integration adoption.",
  "Limitation": "Discards the predecessor’s pull request-specific acceptance versus integration latency separation and explanatory focus on social familiarity and responsiveness.",
  "Observation": "Continuous integration adoption is treated as a natural intervention, with the analysis anchored on identifying the adoption event and comparing longitudinal changes in development practices.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Speciation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Understanding why deep neural network classifiers are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations despite strong generalization on natural inputs.",
  "Mechanism": "A first-order high-dimensional linearity account of adversarial fragility, reframing adversarial examples as a structural consequence of local linearity in modern deep networks.",
  "Limitation": "Standard training objectives favor accuracy on natural data while failing to enforce adversarial robustness.",
  "Observation": "Deep neural network classifiers are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations despite strong generalization on natural inputs.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Speciation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Adapting large language models to follow natural-language instructions and align outputs with human preferences for helpfulness, relevance, and harmlessness.",
  "Mechanism": "Supervised instruction tuning combined with reinforcement learning from a learned reward model trained on pairwise human preference rankings, constrained by KL penalty to stay close to the supervised model.",
  "Limitation": "The predecessor’s emphasis on broad heterogeneous NLP benchmark mixtures reformatted into a unified text-to-text schema is not central in Paper B.",
  "Observation": "Instruction-following behavior is directly optimized for human-preferred outputs rather than only task completion from instructions.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Mutation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Practical Android malware detection directly on end-user mobile devices.",
  "Mechanism": "Lightweight static APK analysis extracting manifest- and code-derived features, then applying a sparse linear classifier with weighted feature presence.",
  "Limitation": "The source paper’s reliance on sandbox execution and runtime event traces is no longer used; B avoids that dependency.",
  "Observation": "Runtime behavior-based clustering or classification is replaced by direct on-device detection using sparse, interpretable static features.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Speciation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Reproducibility audit of benchmark improvements in analytical database evaluation",
  "Mechanism": "A reproducibility-first audit methodology using controlled ablations, matched-capacity baselines, standardized preprocessing and optimization, repeated trials, and stress tests to isolate causal contributions.",
  "Limitation": "The successor no longer retains the predecessor's concern with tradeoffs of a specialized storage/execution co-design for scan-heavy analytical workloads.",
  "Observation": "The successor shifts from ClickHouse-style large-scale analytical querying to determining whether reported benchmark gains are real, robust, and causally attributable.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Speciation"
}
```

```json
{
  "Niche": "Aligning pretrained language models to follow diverse natural-language instructions and improve generalization across varied tasks.",
  "Mechanism": "Self-Instruct is a self-bootstrapping instruction-tuning pipeline generating synthetic instruction-input-output triples from a small seed set, then filtering, deduplicating, and validating them for fine-tuning the same model.",
  "Limitation": "Paper A's dependence on large heterogeneous existing datasets manually rewritten into a unified instruction text-to-text format is discarded.",
  "Observation": "A scaling study of supervised instruction tuning that unifies many NLP tasks as text-to-text instruction–response pairs and shows emergent unseen-task instruction following as model size and instruction diversity increase.",
  "Driver": "Mechanism",
  "Dynamics": "Speciation"
}
```
